title: SACRAMENTO –

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 1906 requires manufacturers to equip smart devices with security features to prevent cyberattacks.",
    "Inadequate security on IoT devices contributed to DDoS attacks by the Mirai BotNet in 2016.",
    "The new law includes a flexible standard for security requirements based on the nature and function of the IoT devices.",
    "In 2016, there were 5 billion IoT devices worldwide.",
    "Industry growth estimates suggest there could be 1 trillion IoT devices by 2025.",
    "Security features for IoT devices are usually less robust than those for traditional computers or smartphones.",
    "Default passwords in IoT devices have allowed millions to become infected with malware.",
    "Infected IoT devices can be used to conduct DDoS attacks without sophisticated hacking skills."
  ]
}
```
